data.title: Comba Telecom Systems Holdings (SEHK:2342) Returns To Profit In 1H 2025 Challenging Bearish Narratives
data.description:

Latest FY 2025 Earnings Snapshot

Comba Telecom Systems Holdings (SEHK:2342) has just posted its FY 2025 first half numbers, with revenue of HK$2,199.2 million and net income of HK$61.8 million, translating into basic EPS of HK$0.021. Looking back over the last three reported half year periods, revenue moved from HK$2,343.5 million in 1H 2024 to HK$2,184.8 million in 2H 2024 and now HK$2,199.2 million in 1H 2025. Net income shifted from a loss of HK$158.4 million and then HK$405.8 million in those earlier halves to the current HK$61.8 million profit. This sets up a results season where the focus is firmly on how durable these margin gains prove to be.

See our full analysis for Comba Telecom Systems Holdings.

Losses to Profit on a 12‑Month View

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Comba moved from a net loss of HK$343.98 million a year earlier to net profit of HK$35.02 million, with Basic EPS shifting from a loss of HK$0.120265 to positive HK$0.0115 over the same period.
  • What stands out for a more optimistic take is that this move into profit lines up with the reported 7.3% per year earnings growth over five years. However, the improvement is still modest when set against earlier losses, which leaves room for bullish investors to ask how repeatable this HK$35.02 million profit really is.
    • Supporters can point to 1H 2025 net income of HK$61.78 million after two loss making halves in FY 2024 of HK$158.43 million and HK$405.76 million, as evidence that the business can at least post profitable periods.
    • At the same time, the fact that trailing 12 month EPS is only HK$0.0115 shows that recent profitability is still relatively thin compared with the size of the revenue base of HK$4,570.83 million.

P/E Of 146x Versus Industry

  • The stock trades on a trailing P/E of 146x, far above both the peer average of 18.7x and the Asian Communications industry average of 36.5x. The current share price of HK$1.64 also sits below the DCF fair value estimate of HK$2.67.
  • Critics highlight that such a high P/E ratio puts a lot of weight on current earnings, yet trailing 12 month net income of HK$35.02 million and EPS of HK$0.0115 are relatively small, which can make the bearish concern about valuation risk feel grounded in the numbers.
    • The contrast between trading about 38.7% below the DCF fair value and at roughly 8x the peer P/E average shows how different valuation tools are giving very different signals.
    • Compared with the industry average P/E of 36.5x, the 146x multiple suggests that even moderate swings in earnings could have a large effect on how comfortable investors feel with the current HK$1.64 price.

Revenue Steady While Profit Swings

  • Across the last three reported halves, revenue stayed in a tight range between HK$2,184.81 million and HK$2,343.46 million, yet net income moved from losses of HK$158.43 million and HK$405.76 million in FY 2024 halves to a profit of HK$61.78 million in 1H 2025.
  • What is striking in the general market view is that relatively stable revenue alongside large profit swings suggests earnings are very sensitive to costs and margins. As a result, investors watching the story may focus more on how that HK$61.78 million profit was achieved than on the small revenue change.
    • The trailing 12 month revenue of HK$4,570.83 million, compared with HK$4,383.97 million a year earlier, shows the top line has stayed around the mid HK$4b level while profitability has moved sharply.
    • Because Basic EPS has ranged from a loss of HK$0.204107 per share on a 12 month view two periods ago to a profit of HK$0.0115 now, the business remains quite sensitive to any shift in operating performance.

data.title: Is Ocugen (OCGN) Pricing Make Sense After Its Volatile Multi‑Year Share Price Swings
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Ocugen's current share price still makes sense after a strong run, the key question is how that price lines up with the underlying value of the business.
  • Ocugen shares, which last closed at US$1.92, have had a mixed but attention grabbing run, with a 21.6% decline over the last 7 days, a 20.0% gain over 30 days, 39.1% year to date, 168.6% over 1 year and 131.3% over 3 years, set against a 71.6% loss over 5 years.
  • Recent news coverage has focused on Ocugen's position in the biotechnology sector and how sentiment around its pipeline and funding outlook may influence risk appetite in the stock. These headlines help explain why the share price has seen sharp moves over short periods, even as longer term performance looks very different depending on the timeframe you focus on.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checklist, Ocugen currently has a value score of 2 out of 6. This means only a minority of the standard valuation checks point to the shares being undervalued. The rest of this article will compare different valuation approaches and then finish with a way of looking at value that can give you a broader view than the usual metrics alone.

Ocugen scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Ocugen Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today using a required rate of return.

For Ocugen, the latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $61.24 million. The model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, which takes analyst inputs where available and then extends them. For example, Simply Wall St uses analyst estimates up to 2030, including a projected free cash flow of $939.25 million in that year, and then extrapolates further figures through to 2035.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of $138.48 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $1.92, this implies an intrinsic discount of 98.6%, which indicates that the shares screen as heavily undervalued under this specific cash flow scenario.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Ocugen is undervalued by 98.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 58 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Ocugen.

Approach 2: Ocugen Price vs Book

For profitable companies, price based multiples are often a quick way to see how much investors are paying for each unit of value. Price to book, or P/B, is especially common for asset heavy or research focused businesses where the balance sheet matters as much as the income statement.

Growth expectations and risk usually influence what looks like a normal P/B multiple. Higher growth and lower perceived risk can support higher P/B ratios, while slower growth or higher risk tends to pull them down.
